series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of an agency, including the
working papers that support the audit.
Also includes audits performed on the
agency.
comments:
The State Auditor's Office
retains any copies of its audits
performed on Texas state
agencies.

series_description:
Including any supporting
documentation created and/or used to
justify and support legislative
appropriations requests by an agency.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final
version of Legislative
Appropriation Requests must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating
the final plan are not subject to
archival review and may be
disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_description:
Non-administrative correspondence
and/or contents received from clients
and/or the general public that require
no action be taken. This includes such
materials as pamphlets and
informational packets. It may include
packages whose contents are trash.
comments:
NOTE: Some packages may be a
health risk and must be disposed
immediately. See also record
series 1.1/AIN 376.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.

Any document that initiates, rescinds,
or amends a regulation, policy, or
procedure that governs the programs,
services, or projects of an agency.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE:
Organizational charts for a single
division, which identify
individual positions but are not
reflective of the overall agency
organization, are not archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the
process of planning new or redefined
programs, services, or projects of an
agency that are not included in or
directly related to other records series
in this schedule.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data
processing planning records are
not archival.

series_description:
Information resources and operational
strategic plans prepared in accordance
with 2054.095 and 2056.002,
Government Code.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final
version of Strategic Plans must
be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating
the final plan are not subject to
archival review and may be
disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_title:
AGENCY RULES,
POLICIES, AND
PROCEDURES -
FINAL
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative
rules, or similar records distributed
internally for the use of employees or
externally to the public or those
individuals or entities regulated by an
agency that sets out the rules, policies,
and procedures that govern an agency's
programs, services, or projects.
comments:
See also records series
1.1071/AIN 308 - Agency Rules,
Policies and Procedures -
Working Files.

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public
relations files maintained or issued by
an agency. Includes print, electronic,
audio, and audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code).

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are exempt under the
Public Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code).

Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other
documents required by law to be
submitted to the Texas Register.

Drafts of proposed legislation and
related correspondence.
Agency submission.

Suggestions submitted by agency
personnel and responses.

Records of temporary usefulness that
are not an integral part of a records
series of an agency, that are not
regularly filed within an agency’s
recordkeeping system, and that are
required only for a limited period of
time for the completion of an action by
an official or employee of the agency or
in the preparation of an ongoing records
series. Transitory records are not
essential to the fulfillment of statutory
obligations or to the documentation of
agency functions. Some examples of
transitory information, which can be in
any medium (voice mail, fax, email,
hard copy, etc.) are routine messages;
telephone message notifications;
internal meeting notices; routing slips;
incoming letters or memoranda of
transmittal that add nothing of
substance to enclosures; and similar
routine information used for
communication, but not for the
documentation, of a specific agency
transaction.
comments:
CAUTION: Records
management officers should use
caution in assigning this record
series to records of an agency to
make certain they are not part of
another records series listed in
this schedule or, for records
series unique to an agency, are
not part of a records series that
documents the fulfillment of the
statutory obligations of the
agency or the documentation of
its functions. The disposal of
transitory information need not
be documented through
destruction signoffs (1.2.001) or
in records disposition logs
(1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part
of its records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_title:
MEETINGS,
CERTIFIED
AGENDAS OR TAPE
RECORDINGS OF
CLOSED MEETINGS
series_description:
Certified agendas or tape recordings of
closed meetings of state boards,
commissions, committees, and councils.
comments:
See caution comment for record
series 1.1.058/AIN 33.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of
state boards, commissions, committees,
and councils from which written
minutes are prepared.
comments:
See caution comment for record
series 1.1.058/AIN 33.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of
state boards, commissions, committees,
and councils including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, etc.
Includes documents sent in advance of
meetings for briefing purposes, some of
which may not be submitted at an
actual meeting.
comments:
See caution comment for record
series 1.1.058/AIN 33.

series_description:
Records of notarizations facilitating
agency business performed by Texas
notary publics while employed by the
OAG.
comments:
NOTE: Original notary public
record books and seals belong to
the notary public and should be
maintained by the notary public
in accordance with rules
established by the Texas
Secretary of State. While
employed by OAG, notary public
record books entries facilitating
agency business are to be
retained for no less than the
retention specified in the
agency's records retention
schedule. Upon separation from
the agency, the notary public
maintains ownership of the
record book and seal, and
provides to the agency copies of
notarizations facilitating agency
business performed while
employed with the agency.
legal_citation:
Government Code
Chapter 406, Civil
Practice and
Remedies Code
Chapter 121; 1
TAC Chapter 87

series_description:
Database of incoming/outgoing phone
calls. Information may include name,
telephone number, reason for the call,
and/or action taken.

Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.

Instructional materials developed by an
agency for training entities or
individuals it regulates or serves.

Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies
as required by Chapter 2004,
Government Code, including quarterly
reports filed with the Texas Ethics
Commission.

Self evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of
the Americans With Disabilities Act.

Any records of an agency needed for
the documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in
an agency's appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency.
comments:
CAUTION: The FE + 3
retention period overrides any
shorter retention period for a
records series in this schedule if
the records series is needed for
documentation of agency
performance measures.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the
governor and legislature as required by
an agency's enabling statutes, including
annual narrative reports if they are
required by statute.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final
version of these agency reports
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3. The
requirement is met by sending
the required copies of the reports
to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports
or studies on non-fiscal aspects of an
agency's programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by
advisory committees, or by consultants
under contract with an agency that are
not noted elsewhere in this schedule.
Includes reports distributed either
internally or to other entities.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: For
agencies that have had an
archival appraisal, separate this
records series by each type of
archival coding, A, R, or E.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to
executive and legislative offices.

comments:
Agency of origin retains the
permanent Record Copy and
sends required copies to the
Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives
Commission and the Legislative
Budget Board.

Send required copies to the
Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives
Commission and the Legislative
Budget Board.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of the
Attorney General on an agency's Public
Information Act activities.

Transcripts and final decisions of
hearings conducted as part of the
regulatory process, and hearings on
proposed rules and changes. The
records may be maintained with related
information including meeting notices,
proofs of publication, and meeting
minutes.

Final agreement described by
Government Code 2009.054(c),
associated with a matter conducted
under an alternative dispute resolution
procedure in which personnel of a state
agency participated as a party on the
agency's behalf.
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies
Code, Chapter
154.071

series_description:
Formal opinions, issued under Section
402.042 or Chapter 552 of the Texas
Government Code, from agency legal
counsel or the Attorney General,
including any requests eliciting the
opinions.
comments:
Original retained permanently by
OAG. Archival requirement to
be met by providing a copy to the
State Archives.

series_description:
Records that are an integral part of the
preparation of formal legal opinions,
that are regularly placed in the formal
legal opinions work files, and that are
required for more than a limited period
of time for the completion of a task
related to the preparation of formal
legal opinions.
comments:
Original retained permanently by
OAG. To be recommended for
archival preservation by State
Archivist & OAG staff upon
annual review.

series_description:
Informal open records letter rulings
issued under Chapter 552 of the Texas
Government Code.
comments:
Original retained permanently by
OAG. Archival requirement to
be met by providing a copy to the
State Archives.

series_description:
Records of “717Q” proceedings dated
prior to Texas Government Code,
Section Chapter 1371.057.
comments:
These are now delivered to the
State Comptroller per Gov’t
Code section 1371.057. But
those dated prior to the
amendment of Chapter 1371 are
still to be retained by OAG until
the end of the 50-year retention
period.

series_description:
Notifications sent to OAG in fulfillment
of the Class Action Fairness Act
(CAFA) where an action is taken by
OAG.
comments:
CAUTION: If OAG does take
action, this notification becomes
part of the case file and is
retained according to that records
series.

series_description:
Amicus briefs on cases that we were
asked to join by another state or our
agency filed.
comments:
Assessed for archival value.
Cases known to have archival
value will be flagged by division,
prior to storage.

series_title:
ATTORNEY NOTES,
DRAFTS, AND
WORKING PAPERS
series_description:
Materials prepared by or for an attorney
in the course of business that include
handwritten notes, draft documents,
research, and working papers that are
not essential to the fulfillment of
statutory obligations or to document
agency functions.
comments:
CAUTION: An attorney may
elect to retain notes, drafts, or
working papers where in his or
her judgment it is desirable for
convenience. However, in most
cases the administrative value of
such materials is temporary as
the materials tend to lose
meaning over time as the creator
has diminished ability to place
the materials in proper context. A
better practice is to either discard
the materials once they have lost
their administrative value or to
have them properly transcribed
into a clearly written
memorandum. This records
series does not include notes that
may appropriately be part of
another records series, such as
1.1, 1.1.061, 1.1.063, 3.1.006,
3.1.014, and 3.1.039.

series_description:
Information received in the information
gathering or discovery process that was
not used in the case or investigation.
comments:
Once the case or investigation is
closed, this information may be
processed through the
appropriate disposition process.

Record must be kept for the
duration of the retention of the
corresponding litigation file.

series_description:
Synopsis of several cases of a similar
nature.

comments:
Original microfilm stored at
Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, duplicate
retained by agency.

Original microfilm stored at
Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, duplicate
retained by agency.

series_description:
Consumer complaints and related
documents received by the Consumer
Protection Division of the OAG.
Complaint data maintained in the
consumer complaint database.

Copy of any complaint filed by any
person or governmental entity under an
antitrust statute.

LITIGATION FILES
AND

provided their retrieval rate can
be accommodated.

Cases known to have archival

Litigation that involves crimes against
children.

52.006.
DURATION OF
LIEN (B)

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an

comments:
Collections - Litigation cases

archival or historical value by the
State Archives.

series_description:
Files dealing with Land and/or Oil
Disputes with Statewide or Multi-State
Impact, including River Compact
Commission files and related “Water
Rights” cases and Open Beaches cases.
comments:
If the agency decides the files are
no longer of permanent value,
the records will be assessed for
long-term value and then
transferred to the State Archives
provided their retrieval rate can
be accommodated.

series_title:
ENVIRONMENTAL
ENFORCEMENT
CASES CONCERNING
CONTAMINATED
SITES
comments:
Assessed for archival value.
Cases known to have archival
value will be flagged by division,
prior to storage.

series_title:
LITIGATION -
ENVIRONMENTAL
PROTECTION/ABSTR
ACTS AND
PERMANENT
INJUNCTIONS
series_description:
Cases wherein the defendant owes
money to the state, an abstract of
judgment is on file, and/or a permanent
injunction is in a judgment.

Cases wherein the defendant owes
money to the Texas Department of
Transportation.
comments:
A Judgment is filed with the
Court and an Abstract of
Judgment is filed with the
County Clerk. The Judgment
Lien will remain active for a
period of 20 years with the
option to renew for one
additional 20-year period.

NOTE: Case must be reopened
and re-closed in the OAG Case
Management System.

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of the
Medicaid Fraud Control Unit (MFCU)
in the adjudication of a lawsuit
involving violations of state or federal
laws, including but not limited to cases
regarding fraud in the administration of
the Medicaid program, the provision of
medical assistance, the activities of
entities, providers, or individuals under
the state Medicaid program; or the
abuse, neglect or exploitation of
individuals in institutional or non-
institutional settings over which MFCU
has investigatory authority.

series_title:
PUBLIC FINANCE
DIVISION - PUBLIC
SECURITY WORK
FILES
series_description:
Work papers involving the Public
Finance Division’s review and approval
of public security transcripts.
comments:
NOTE: Controversial bond
financings may have
administrative need and value
beyond 15 years. If the
administrative value is
determined to not be beyond the
15-year minimum, these records
may be stored with the OAG
Records Center for the last 5
years of the minimum retention,
after having been in the division
10 years.

series_title:
PUBLIC FINANCE
DIVISION -
MONTHLY
TRANSCRIPT
ACTIVITY REPORTS
series_description:
Reports compiled on a monthly basis
pertaining to the fees and the number of

Hardbound compilations of opinions

transcripts of proceedings submitted to

Incoming/outgoing and internal
correspondence pertaining to the Public
Finance Division’s review and approval
of transcripts of proceedings
authorizing bonds or similar
obligations.

legal_citation:
Code of Criminal
Procedure Articles
56.06 and 56.065

comments:
NOTE: OAG has been granted a
permitted exception by the Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission for this records series,
pursuant to Government Code,
Section 441.191(a). The permitted
exception authorizes the OAG to
return original records received by
the OAG pursuant to civil
investigative demand (CID) under
Texas Business and Commerce
Code, Section 17.61 or Texas
Human Resource Code, Section
36.054 to the provider. Records
received by the OAG pursuant to
CID that are not necessary to
conduct agency business may be
returned to the provider without
making copies of these records.
Records necessary to conduct
agency business are to be copied,
and the copies are to be retained by
the agency according to the OAG
RRS. Once copies have been made,
the originals of the records necessary
to conduct agency business may be
returned to the provider.

NOTE: OAG has been granted a
permitted exception by the Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission for this records series,
pursuant to Government Code,
Section 441.191(a). The permitted
exception authorizes the OAG to
return original records received by
the OAG pursuant to civil
investigative demand (CID) under
Texas Business and Commerce
Code, Section 17.61 or Texas
Human Resource Code, Section
36.054 to the provider. Records
received by the OAG pursuant to
CID that are not necessary to
conduct agency business may be
returned to the provider without
making copies of these records.
Records necessary to conduct
agency business are to be copied,
and the copies are to be retained by
the agency according to the OAG
RRS. Once copies have been made,
the originals of the records necessary
to conduct agency business may be
returned to the provider.

Agency copy.

Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists,
registers, guides, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: These records must
carry the same retention period
and archival code of the records
they support.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.
Agency copy of form.

Also includes documentation for
disaster recovery services provided by
other entities.

Background material, copy (drafts),
original artwork, photo negatives,
prints, flats, etc. This includes all work
performed both inside and outside the
agency.

Machine-readable files used in the
creation, utilization, and updating of
master files.
comments:
CAUTION: Records
Management Officers must be
certain before assigning the
retention period of AC to agency
automated processing files that
the files do not fall under records
series listed elsewhere.

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent
sets of complete and accurate electronic
records. Examples include, but are not
limited to, data tables of relational
databases used by applications or
computer programs.
comments:
CAUTION: Records
Management Officers must be
certain before assigning the
retention period of AC to agency
automated processing files that
the files do not fall under records
series listed elsewhere.

series_description:
Automated software applications and
operating system files including job
control language, program
listing/source code, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system
requirements, hardware configurations,
and equipment control systems.
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all
technical characteristics necessary for
reading or processing of electronic
records and their timely, authorized
disposition including documentation
describing how a system operates and
necessary for using the system such as
user guides, system or sub-system
definitions, system specifications, input
and output specifications, and system
flow charts; program descriptions and
documentation such as program
flowcharts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, and other records that
document modifications to computer
programs; and data documentation
necessary to access, retrieve,
manipulate, and interpret data in an
automated system such as a data
element dictionary, file layout, code
book or table, and other records that
explain the meaning, purpose, structure,
logical relationships, and origin of the
data elements.
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits
such as files or reports showing
transactions accepted, rejected,
suspended, and/or processed; history
files/tapes; records of online updates to
application files or security logs.

series_title:
IRS - ELECTRONIC
SECURITY AUDIT
TRAIL LOGS
series_description:
Computer generated records that must
contain information that establishes
what type of event occurred, when the
event occurred, where the event
occurred, the source of the event, the
outcome of the event, and the identity
of any individual or subjects associated
with the event. Records must contain
details to facilitate the reconstruction of
events if unauthorized activity or a
malfunction occurs or is suspected in
the audit records for audit events
identified by type, location, or subject.
legal_citation:
Texas Code of
Criminal Procedure,
Chapter 56, Article
56.06

series_title:
FINDING AIDS,
INDEXES AND
TRACKING SYSTEMS
series_description:
Automated indexes, lists, registers, and
other finding aids used to provide
access to records.
comments:
CAUTION: These records must
carry the same retention period
and archival code of the records
they support.

series_description:
Forms and logs used to reconcile
batches submitted for processing
against batches received and processed.
comments:
Retention established by agency
directive.

series_description:
Reference mainframe program jobs.
Natural request for production changes.
Natural Program listings.

Reports showing transactions that were
accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or
processed.

Electronic files or automated logs
created to monitor computer systems
such as print spool logs, console logs,
tape activity logs, etc.

series_title:
CHARGEBACK
RECORDS TO DATA
PROCESSING
SERVICES USERS
series_description:
Records used to document, calculate
costs, and bill program units for
computer usage and data processing
services. These records are also used
for cost recovery, budgeting, or
administrative purposes.

Schedules or similar records showing
computer jobs to be run and other
reports by computer operators or
programmers of work performed.

Information verifying the quality of
system, hardware, or software
operations including records of errors
or failures and the loss of data resulting
from such failures, documentation of
abnormal termination and of error free
processing, checks of changes put into
production, transaction histories, and
other records needed as an audit trail to
evaluate data accuracy.

Data resident on hard drives that make
use of user specific information
transmitted by the Web server onto the
user's computer so that the information
might be available for later access by
itself or other servers.
comments:
The disposal of Internet cookies
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations
(1.2.001) or in records
disposition logs (1.2.010), but
agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its
records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_description:
A record of the documents visited
during an Internet session that allows
users to access previously visited pages
more quickly or to generate a record of
usage of a state-owned computer.
comments:
The disposal of history files need
not be documented through
destruction authorizations
(1.2.001) or in records
disposition logs (1.2.010), but
agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its
records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_title:
SOFTWARE
REGISTRATIONS,
WARRANTIES, AND
LICENSE
AGREEMENTS

series_description:
Employee Application, Performance
Appraisals, Personnel Disciplinary
Action Documentation, Personnel
Information or Action Forms, Personnel
Data Change Form, Applicant
Acknowledgment, New Employee
Information, Verification of
Employment Eligibility, Employee
Clearance Check List, Personnel File
Release, Aptitude and Skills Tests (Test
Papers).
comments:
Retained in Human Resources
Division.

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts,
letters of reference, and similar
documents whose submission by
candidates for vacant positions is
required on the application form, by
application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement. Applicant
Tracking Form, Aptitude and Skills
Tests.

Notes, memoranda, or reports relating
to the counseling of an employee for
work-related, personal, or substance
abuse problems. Usually maintained at
the supervisorial level except in those
agencies with counseling staff.
comments: Retained at division level.

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating
to the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, and other types of
insurance offered by the State of Texas
to its employees.
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that
serve as payroll deduction
authorizations must be
maintained for the retention
period prescribed for record
series 3.2.001/AIN 157A.

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating
to the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, and other types of
insurance offered by the State of Texas
to its employees. Includes Workers
Compensation and Sick Leave Pool
Documentation.
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that
serve as payroll deduction
authorizations must be
maintained for the retention
period prescribed for record
series 3.2.001/AIN 157A.

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating
to requests for protected leave under
the Federal Family and Medical Leave
Act (FMLA) and the state Non-FMLA
Parental Leave statute.

Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

EEOC/TCHR notice of discrimination
requested information, correspondence,
agency’s position statement/response,
supporting documentation.

Voluntary comments on reason for

Job descriptions, including all
associated task or skill statements, for

Criminal history record information on
job applicants or agency employees
obtained from the Department of Public
Safety (DPS).
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or See Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code
for appropriate retention and use
of this information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, transcripts,
test scores, or similar records
documenting the training, testing, or
continuing education achievements of
an employee.
Federal reporting form (INS I-9).
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS I-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever later.
Agencies should make certain
that the INS I-9 forms for
employees who terminate from
an agency less than 3 years from
date of hire are kept for the 3
year retention period.

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating
to the selection of available benefit
options other than insurance.
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that
serve as payroll deduction
authorizations must be
maintained for the retention
period prescribed for record
series 3.2.001/AIN 157A.

series_description:
Summary of applicant qualifications,
evaluation basis for selection or
rejection, original applications, job
assignments, promotions, separations,
layoffs, terminations, compensation
records, conditions of work, time
records, hours of training provided
(separate from labor), minority status,
and gender of all selected and rejected
applicants.

Voluntary information asked of
volunteers and/or interns.
Awards, incentives, tenure, or related.

Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required
deductions from payroll, including
garnishment or other court-ordered
attachments.

Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.

Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement,
or in the computation of taxes.

Employer’s copy of “Employee’s
Withholding Exemption Certificate.”

Notice of application for unemployment
benefits, determination on payment of
unemployment, wage verification
notices.
457 & 401(k) agreements, policies, etc.
comments:
For instructions regarding the
determination of the closure of
accounts and for additional
information regarding the
retention period see the most
current edition of the Benefits
Coordinator Reference Manual
issued by the Employees
Retirement System of Texas.

series_description: Includes supporting documentation.
comments:
Agency copy is original; must
remain on file at agency.

series_description:
Documents pertaining to the hiring of
temporary staff.

General

(H.B. No. 1)

11.18. (76th

Rights Act of 1964-

Employee benefit plans such as

Reports providing statistical

Records relating to review and

Work, duty, shift, crew, or case
schedules, rosters, or assignments.

series_description:

predecessor pertaining to employees.

ACTIVITIES,
REPORTS AND
AUTHORIZATIONS
TO ENGAGE IN
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for
other work-related activities for which
the expenses of the employee are
defrayed or reimbursed.

Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that
define agency wide policies and
procedures concerning the personnel of
an agency.

Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position-by position
basis.

Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including

staff hired during a month, detailed
listings of employees within its
organizational structure, position
vacancies, analyses of turnover rates
and seasonality of employment, etc.

Aptitude or skills tests required of job
applicants or of current personnel to
qualify for promotion or transfer.

CAUTION: One copy of each
different test (different in terms
of either questions or
administration procedures)
should be retained for the period
indicated.

series_description:
Records of the validation of aptitude
and skills tests.

Includes documentation used to
complete EEO reports.

Reports, studies, aggregated or
summary data, and similar
documentation compiled to monitor and
demonstrate compliance with the
federal Equal Pay Act.

Used to create employee leave
balances, to transfer leave balances
when an employee transfers positions,
to adjust carry-over balances on August
31, to correct errors on leave
accumulation, and to close out leave
accounts on separated employees.

DOCUMENTATION
AND STATUS

series_description:

Summaries and documentation to

Paper medium retained AC+1

records are appropriately
destroyed.

series_description:
One for all accounts in use for a fiscal
year.

GRANTS
CONTRACTS AND
GRANT FILES,
INCLUDING

series_description:
This series documents state and federal
grant projects participated in or
administered by state agencies. It
includes grant authorization records,
which provide evidence of the award of
grants to or by agencies; grant/project
financial or performance reports, which
are periodic reports of financial activity
and/or program performance related to
grants received or made by agencies.
Associated with a grant executed,
renewed, or amended on or before
August 31, 2015.
comments:
CAUTION: Retention
requirements may vary
depending on the specific federal
funding agency. Agencies must
ensure that records are retained
for the appropriate retention
period.

series_description:
This series documents state and federal
grant projects participated in or
administered by state agencies. It
includes grant authorization records,
which provide evidence of the award of
grants to or by agencies; grant/project
financial or performance reports, which
are periodic reports of financial activity
and/or program performance related to
grants received or made by agencies.
Associated with a grant executed,
renewed, or amended on or after
September 1, 2015.
comments:
CAUTION: Retention
requirements may vary
depending on the specific federal
funding agency. Agencies must
ensure that records are retained
for the appropriate retention
period.

series_description: Bonds, etc.

Signature cards or similar records

Any mailing address, telephone or fax

Records and reports of postage

For vehicles, equipment, etc. Executed,

For vehicles, equipment, etc. Executed,

Any internally distributed manual,

Supply orders, reports, etc.

Shows additions, changes, transfers,

Reservation logs or similar records

Log, register, etc., providing a record of

Daily/weekly intelligence logs.

series_title:
IRS - FTI ACCESS
RECORDS AND LOGS
series_description:
Records include but are not limited to:
logs tracking the location of electronic
and non-electronic records with FTI;
logs tracking records with FTI
disclosed outside the agency; Visitor
and Building Access Logs including
Authorized Access List (AAL)
Disclosure Awareness Certification.

The data related to employee ID
Cards/Key Cards.

Accident or occupational disease
reports (by supervisors and employees)
and other associated reports required to
be submitted to the Texas Department
of Insurance or its predecessors or
maintained internally on accident
frequency.
comments:
The Texas Department of
Insurance retains copies of the
reports submitted to it for 50
years.

series_description:
Plans for evacuation of agency facilities
in cases of emergency.

Fire, safety, and other inspection
records of agency facilities and
equipment. IRS requires documented
inspections to include Inspection Cycle,
Safeguard Security Report (SSR) and a
record of corrective actions, if any.
comments:
IRS requires all inspections with
corrective actions be maintained
for five (5) years after close. IRS
Publication 1075 (Dec. 2013),
Section 6.4. CAUTION: Does
not include inspection reports of
building construction. See record
series 5.2.028. Note: These
records constitute official IRS
agency records and may not be
disclosed outside of the agency.
Any request for the release of
IRS records is subject to
disclosure restrictions under the
federal law and IRS rules and
regulations.

Internal listing of all incoming/outgoing

Documentation relating to disputed

use.
